functions.label: generateToken
description:
Generates a deterministic anonymization token for a field value.

The token format is `<ENTITY_CLASS>_<HASH>` where the hash is derived from
a per-space secret using HMAC-SHA256.

The same (secret, entityClass, field, value) tuple always produces the same
token, ensuring stability within a space. Different spaces use different
secrets, preventing cross-space token correlation.
signature: (secret: string, entityClass: string, field: string, value: string, hashLength?: number) => string

children.label: secret
description: - Per-space secret material for HMAC (must be non-empty)

children.label: entityClass
description: - Token prefix/class label (e.g., `HOST_NAME`, `USER_NAME`)

children.label: field
description: - The field name being anonymized (e.g., `host.name`)

children.label: value
description: - The original field value to anonymize

children.label: hashLength
description: - Number of hex chars to include (default: 32, clamped to 1–64)

functions.label: replaceTokensWithOriginals
description:
Replaces anonymization tokens in a text string with their original values
using the provided token-to-original mapping.

This is a pure function usable by both client and server code.
The UI should use this for local deanonymization when it already has
an authorized mapping, rather than calling the server for substitution.

Tokens are replaced longest-first to avoid partial replacement issues
(e.g., `HOST_NAME_abc` should not be partially matched by a shorter token).

children.label: text
description: - The text containing anonymization tokens

children.label: tokenToOriginal
description: - Map of token → original value

returnComment: The text with tokens replaced by original values

functions.label: resolveEffectivePolicy
description:
Resolves the effective anonymization policy from one or more sets of field rules.

When multiple profiles contribute rules for the same field, conflicts are
resolved using "most restrictive wins":

- **Deny** (`allowed=false`) wins over everything
- **Anonymize** (`allowed=true`, `anonymized=true`) wins over Allow
- **Allow** (`allowed=true`, `anonymized=false`)

This is a pure function that can be used by both the anonymization plugin's
service layer and unit tests.
signature: (...fieldRuleSets: { field: string; allowed: boolean; anonymized: boolean; entityClass: any; }[][]) =>

children.label: fieldRuleSets
description: - One or more arrays of field rules (from contributing profiles)
signature: { field: string; allowed: boolean; anonymized: boolean; entityClass: any; }[][]

returnComment: The effective policy keyed by field name

functions.label: suggestEntityClassForField
description:
Returns the suggested `AnonymizationEntityClass` for a given ECS field path,
or `undefined` if no mapping is known for that field.

Intended for two use cases:
 1. UI suggestions — auto-populate the entity class picker when a user adds a
    field to a profile.
 2. Programmatic profile initialization — populate `entityClass` on default
    field rules without hard-coding the mapping in the consumer.

misc.label: AnonymizationProfile
description:
An Anonymization Profile: a reusable policy configuration scoped to a target
(data view, index pattern, or index) within a Kibana space.
signature: { id: string; name: string; targetType: any; targetId: string; rules: { fieldRules: { field: string; allowed: boolean; anonymized: boolean; entityClass: any; }[]; regexRules: { id: string; type: any; entityClass: any; pattern: string; enabled: boolean; }[]; nerRules: { id: string; type: any; allowedEntityClasses: any[]; enabled: boolean; modelId?: string | undefined; }[]; }; saltId: string; namespace: string; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; createdBy: string; updatedBy: string; description?: string | undefined; }

signature: { fieldRules: { field: string; allowed: boolean; anonymized: boolean; entityClass: any; }[]; regexRules: { id: string; type: any; entityClass: any; pattern: string; enabled: boolean; }[]; nerRules: { id: string; type: any; allowedEntityClasses: any[]; enabled: boolean; modelId?: string | undefined; }[]; }

misc.label: EffectiveFieldPolicy
description: The effective policy state for a single field after resolution.

misc.label: EffectivePolicy
description:
The effective policy for all fields after resolving one or more profiles.
Keyed by field name.

misc.label: FieldRule
description:
A per-field rule specifying whether the field is allowed as context,
and whether its value should be anonymized (tokenized).
signature: { field: string; allowed: boolean; anonymized: boolean; entityClass: any; }

misc.label: NER_MODEL_ID
description: default NER model

misc.label: NerRule
description:
A Named Entity Recognition (NER) rule that uses an ML model to detect entities.
Only applied when a trusted NER model is configured and available.
signature: { id: string; type: any; allowedEntityClasses: any[]; enabled: boolean; modelId?: string | undefined; }

misc.label: RegexRule
description: A regex-based anonymization rule that matches patterns in free text.
signature: { id: string; type: any; entityClass: any; pattern: string; enabled: boolean; }

misc.label: ReplacementsSet
description: A replacements set persisted per namespace.
signature: { id: string; namespace: string; replacements: { anonymized: string; original: string; }[]; createdAt: string; updatedAt: string; createdBy: string; }

misc.label: TokenSourceEntry
description: A single token source entry describing where a token was produced.
signature: { token: string; pointer: string; entityClass: any; sourceType: any; sourceId: string; sourceRef?: Record<string, any> | undefined; spanStart?: number | undefined; spanEnd?: number | undefined; field?: string | undefined; fieldRef?: string | undefined; ruleType?: string | undefined; ruleId?: string | undefined; firstSeenAt?: string | undefined; }

objects.label: ANONYMIZATION_ENTITY_CLASSES
description:
Full canonical set of anonymization entity class labels.
Includes NER classes plus domain-specific field labels.
This list intentionally stays compact/canonical and does not include a
dedicated `PHONE` class; phone-like regex matches should use the closest
canonical class (`MISC`) until/unless a formal class is added.
Use these as token prefixes in masks (e.g. `HOST_NAME_abc123`).

objects.label: anonymizationProfileRulesSchema
description: Reusable profile rules payload shared by APIs and persisted profiles.

objects.label: createAnonymizationProfileRequestSchema
description: Public API payload for profile creation.

objects.label: ECS_ENTITY_CLASS_MAP
description:
Maps known ECS (and Kibana-specific) field paths to their canonical
anonymization entity class. Used to auto-suggest entity classes in the UI
when a user is building a new anonymization profile, and to populate
default field rules when profiles are automatically initialized.

Fields not present in this map should be left to the user to classify.

children.label: 'source.ip'
description: // IP addresses

children.label: 'host.name'
description: // Host / machine identifiers

children.label: 'user.name'
description: // User identifiers

children.label: 'url.full'
description: // URLs

children.label: 'user.email'
description: // Email addresses

children.label: 'cloud.account.id'
description: // Cloud account

children.label: 'entity.name'
description: // Generic named entity (Kibana-specific ECS extension)

children.label: 'resource.name'
description: // Resource (Kibana-specific ECS extension)

children.label: 'organization.name'
description: // Organizations → CoNLL-03 ORG class

children.label: 'user.changes.full_name'
description: // People → CoNLL-03 PER class

children.label: 'geo.city_name'
description: // Locations → CoNLL-03 LOC class

objects.label: findAnonymizationProfilesQuerySchema
description: Public API query payload for profile search.

objects.label: NER_ENTITY_CLASSES
description:
Entity classes produced by NER models (CoNLL-03 standard classes).
These are the only classes a NER model may emit; used to filter model output.

objects.label: updateAnonymizationProfileRequestSchema
description: Public API payload for profile updates.
